text: This page covers your profile, password, active sessions, and the organization-level security controls on Team and Business plans. Two-factor authentication has its own dedicated guide. Edit your name, photo, and preferred time zone under **Settings → Profile**. Your time zone affects reminder delivery, due-date display, and email digest timing. Passwords must be at least **10 characters**. Change yours under **Settings → Security → Password**; you'll be asked for your current password first. Changing your password signs out all other sessions automatically. If you sign in only with Google or Apple, you don't have a Tessera password unless you choose to set one. **Settings → Security → Active sessions** lists every device currently signed in to your account, with its browser or app, approximate location, and last-active time. Click **Sign out** next to any session to end it, or **Sign out everywhere** to end all sessions except the one you're using. Sessions expire automatically after **30 days** of inactivity. Signing out of a session immediately revokes its access. Tessera supports **passkeys** (WebAuthn) for passwordless sign-in using your device's fingerprint, face, or hardware security key. Add a passkey under **Settings → Security → Passkeys**. Passkeys can be used on their own or as a second factor. On the **Business** plan, administrators can enforce **SAML-based SSO** with identity providers such as Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, or Google Workspace. Configure it under **Admin console → Security → SSO**. When SSO is enforced, members sign in through your identity provider and local passwords are disabled. Business organizations can connect **SCIM** to automatically create, update, and deactivate member accounts from their identity provider, so removing someone there immediately revokes their Tessera access. Team and Business owners and admins manage members, roles, security policy, and billing from the **Admin console**. From here you can require two-factor authentication for all members, set a session length policy, and review the audit log. The **audit log** records sign-ins, permission changes, space creation and deletion, exports, and member changes. Team retains **90 days** of audit history; Business retains **1 year**. Export the log as CSV from the admin console. Delete your account under **Settings → Account → Delete account**. This is permanent and removes your personal space and content after a **30-day** grace period, during which you can still recover it by signing back in.

Tessera offers four plans. This page lists what each includes and how billing works. Prices are in US dollars. For individuals getting started. Includes your personal space plus up to **2 shared spaces** (max **3 members** each), up to **1,000 notes** total, **2 GB** of storage, **50 MB** maximum file upload, **7-day** version history, and the built-in template gallery. Community support only. For power users. Everything in Free plus **unlimited notes**, **unlimited shared spaces**, **50 GB** storage, **250 MB** file uploads, **90-day** version history, custom templates, the graph view, and advanced search operators. Priority email support. For teams that collaborate daily. Everything in Plus plus up to **250 members** per space, **unlimited** version history, guest access, roles and permissions, the admin console, and a **90-day** audit log. Pooled storage of **100 GB per member**. For organizations with security and compliance needs. Everything in Team plus **SSO / SAML**, **SCIM** provisioning, domain capture, a **1-year** audit log, configurable data retention, **500 MB** file uploads, and pooled storage of **1 TB per member**. Priority support with a one-business-day response target. For larger or regulated deployments, **Enterprise** terms are available — contact **sales@tessera.app**. Choose monthly or annual billing. **Annual billing saves roughly two months** compared with paying monthly (about a 17% discount). You can switch between monthly and annual at any time; the change takes effect on your next renewal. Paid plans are billed per **active member** across your organization. Adding a member mid-cycle is **prorated** for the days remaining; removing a member credits the unused time toward your next invoice. Guests and viewers on public links are **not** counted as billable members. We accept major credit and debit cards and, on annual Team and Business plans, payment by invoice (ACH or wire). Manage your card and billing email under **Settings → Billing**. Upgrade instantly from **Settings → Billing → Change plan**; new features unlock right away. Downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing period so you keep what you paid for. If a downgrade would put you over a limit (for example, too many shared spaces), Tessera asks you to resolve that first. Monthly plans are not refunded for partial months. Annual plans can be **refunded within 14 days** of purchase or renewal. Request a refund from **Settings → Billing → Help**, or email **support@tessera.app**. Every payment generates a receipt, emailed to your billing address and available under **Settings → Billing → Invoices**. Add a company name, address, and VAT/Tax ID to appear on invoices.

Comments let your team discuss work in context without cluttering the note itself. Mentions pull the right people into that discussion. Select any text and click the **comment** bubble that appears, or press **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + M**. Your comment anchors to that selection and shows up as a highlight other readers can click. You can also comment on an image, a table, or a whole note rather than a specific selection. Each comment starts a **thread**. Reply inline to keep a conversation together. Threads show the author, timestamp, and edit history of each reply. Type `@` followed by a name to mention a teammate in a comment or note body. The person receives a notification linking directly to the comment. You can mention anyone who is a member or guest of the current space. When a discussion is finished, click **Resolve**. Resolved comments are hidden from the note but not deleted — reopen them anytime from the **Resolved** filter in the comments panel. Resolving a comment notifies everyone in the thread. Add an emoji **reaction** to any comment to acknowledge it quickly without adding a reply. Reactions are a lightweight way to signal agreement or that you've read something. Open the **Comments** tab in the right context panel to see every open thread in a note, sorted by position. A number badge on the panel shows how many unresolved threads remain. By default you are notified when someone replies to a thread you started or participated in, or mentions you directly. Tune this under **Settings → Notifications**, where you can choose in-app, email, and push delivery independently for mentions, replies, and assignments. Adding comments requires at least the **Commenter** role. **Viewers** can read existing comments but cannot post. Anyone with **Editor** or **Admin** rights can delete any comment; everyone else can delete only their own.

You own your content in Tessera. This page explains how to export it, how long we keep data, and how we handle privacy. Open a note's **⋯** menu and choose **Export**. A note can be exported as **Markdown (.md)**, **PDF**, or **HTML**. Markdown export preserves headings, links, lists, and code blocks. Attachments are included as linked files in Markdown and HTML exports. Space admins can export an entire space from **Space settings → Export**. Choose a format: - **Markdown** — a ZIP of `.md` files mirroring your folder structure, plus an `attachments/` directory. - **JSON** — a structured export including notes, tasks, comments, and metadata, suitable for programmatic import elsewhere. - **PDF** — one PDF per note, bundled in a ZIP. Large exports are prepared in the background; you'll get an Inbox notification and an email with a download link when the file is ready. Download links expire after **72 hours**. Request a full export of everything you own from **Settings → Account → Export all data**. This produces a single ZIP containing every space you administer plus your personal space. - **Trash:** deleted notes are recoverable for **30 days**, then permanently removed. - **Version history:** kept according to your plan (7 days on Free, 90 days on Plus, unlimited on Team and Business). - **Deleted account:** content is permanently erased **30 days** after deletion. - **Backups:** encrypted backups are retained for **35 days** for disaster recovery, after which they are rotated out. Content is stored on infrastructure in the United States and the European Union. **Business** customers can choose an EU-only data residency region during onboarding. Data is encrypted **in transit** with TLS 1.2 or higher and **at rest** with AES-256. We do not sell your data and we do not use your private notes to train machine-learning models. You can request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal data by emailing **privacy@tessera.app**. We aim to respond within **30 days**, consistent with GDPR and CCPA. We use a small set of vetted sub-processors for hosting, email delivery, and payment processing. The current list is published at **tessera.app/legal/subprocessors** and customers can subscribe to be notified of changes.

Short answers to the questions we hear most. For detail, follow the link in each answer to the full guide. Yes. The **Free** plan costs $0 forever and includes your personal space, up to 2 shared spaces, 1,000 notes, and 2 GB of storage. See **Billing and Plans** for the full comparison. Yes, on the mobile and desktop apps. Recently opened notes are cached automatically, and you can mark a space as **Available offline** for full local access. Edits sync when you reconnect. Export any note as Markdown, PDF, or HTML, or export a whole space as Markdown, JSON, or PDF from **Space settings → Export**. You're never locked in. See **Data Export and Privacy**. No. We do not use your private content to train machine-learning models, and we do not sell your data. See **Data Export and Privacy**. Your account drops to the Free plan if you downgrade, or, if you delete the account, content is permanently erased after a 30-day grace period. You can export everything first. Yes, on the **Team** and **Business** plans an admin can enforce 2FA for all members from the admin console. See **Two-Factor Authentication**. Yes. The REST API at **api.tessera.app/v1** lets you read and write notes, tasks, and spaces with a personal access token. See the **REST API Reference**. The latest two versions of Chrome, Edge, Firefox, and Safari. Older browsers may work but aren't officially supported. Email **support@tessera.app**, or ask the community at **community.tessera.app**. Check **status.tessera.app** first if you suspect an outage. Admins can rename an organization and set a custom workspace slug under the admin console. Personal-space names can be changed anytime in settings.

Tessera is a connected workspace where your notes, tasks, and team knowledge live side by side. This guide takes you from sign-up to your first shared space in about ten minutes. 1. Go to **app.tessera.app** and choose **Sign up**. 2. Register with an email address and a password (minimum 10 characters), or continue with Google or Apple. 3. Verify your email by clicking the link we send. Unverified accounts can read and write but cannot invite collaborators. Every new account starts on the **Free** plan. You can upgrade at any time from **Settings → Billing**. The app has three regions: - **Sidebar** (left): your spaces, pinned notes, and the **Tasks**, **Search**, and **Inbox** entries. - **Editor** (center): the note or board you are currently viewing. - **Context panel** (right): backlinks, comments, and version history for the open note. Press **Cmd/Ctrl + K** anywhere to open the command palette, the fastest way to jump between notes, run actions, or create something new. 1. Click **+ New note** at the top of the sidebar, or press **Cmd/Ctrl + N**. 2. Give it an H1 title on the first line — this becomes the note's name in the sidebar. 3. Start typing. Use Markdown shortcuts such as `#` for a heading, `-` for a bullet, `[]` for a checkbox, and `[[` to link to another note. Notes save automatically every few seconds. There is no **Save** button. Spaces group related notes and tasks. New accounts come with one **Personal** space. 1. Click the **+** next to **Spaces** in the sidebar. 2. Name the space (for example, "Marketing") and pick an icon. 3. Invite teammates by email. They receive an invitation link that expires after 7 days. On the Free plan you can create up to **2 shared spaces** with up to **3 members** each. Paid plans remove these limits. - Connect an integration such as Slack or Google Calendar from **Settings → Integrations**. - Install the mobile app for iOS or Android to capture notes on the go. - Turn on two-factor authentication under **Settings → Security**. If you get stuck, email **support@tessera.app** or visit the community forum at **community.tessera.app**.

Connect Tessera to the other tools your team uses. Manage every connection under **Settings → Integrations**. Most integrations require the **Plus** plan or higher; Slack and the Web Clipper work on every plan. <!-- Screenshots for each integration are added in the design pass; placeholders omitted here. --> Link a Slack workspace to: - Receive notifications for mentions, assignments, and comments in chosen Slack channels. - Use the **/tessera** slash command to capture a quick note or task into a space without leaving Slack. - Unfurl Tessera links pasted in Slack into rich previews showing the note title and a snippet. Connect from **Settings → Integrations → Slack** and authorize the workspace. Show tasks that have due dates on your Google Calendar. The sync is **one-way**, from Tessera to Calendar: completing or rescheduling a task updates the calendar event, but editing the event in Google does not change the task. Choose which spaces feed the calendar during setup. Attach Google Drive files to a note as live embeds. Embedded Drive files show their current title and thumbnail and open in Drive when clicked. Tessera never copies the file's contents; it stores only a link, so Drive permissions still apply. The Zapier integration exposes triggers (such as **New note**, **Task completed**) and actions (such as **Create note**, **Create task**) so you can automate workflows across thousands of apps without writing code. Send real-time events to your own endpoint. Create a webhook under **Settings → Integrations → Webhooks** and subscribe to events including `note.created`, `note.updated`, `note.deleted`, `task.created`, `task.completed`, and `comment.created`. Each delivery is signed with an `X-Tessera-Signature` header (HMAC-SHA256) you can verify with your webhook's secret. Failed deliveries retry with exponential backoff for up to 24 hours. The **Tessera Web Clipper** browser extension saves web pages, selections, and bookmarks straight into a space. It's available for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. Clip a full article as clean Markdown, a simplified reader view, or a URL bookmark with a screenshot. Bring existing content in from **Settings → Import**. Tessera imports from **Markdown** files and folders, **Evernote** (`.enex`), **Notion** (Markdown/CSV export), and plain **CSV** for tasks. Imports preserve folder structure where possible and run in the background.

Tessera is built to be driven from the keyboard. On macOS use **Cmd**; on Windows and Linux use **Ctrl**. View this list anytime by pressing **?**. - **Cmd/Ctrl + K** — open the command palette - **Cmd/Ctrl + N** — new note - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + F** — full search - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + I** — open the Inbox - **Cmd/Ctrl + ,** — open settings - **?** — show this shortcuts list - **Cmd/Ctrl + \\** — toggle the sidebar - **Cmd/Ctrl + .** — toggle focus mode - **Cmd/Ctrl + [** — go back - **Cmd/Ctrl + ]** — go forward - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + O** — jump to a heading in the current note (outline) - **Cmd/Ctrl + B** — bold - **Cmd/Ctrl + I** — italic - **Cmd/Ctrl + U** — underline - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + S** — strikethrough - **Cmd/Ctrl + L** — turn the selection into a link - **Cmd/Ctrl + E** — inline code - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + C** — copy the current code block - **/** — open the slash menu to insert a block - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + ↑ / ↓** — move the current block up or down - **Cmd/Ctrl + D** — duplicate the current block - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + Backspace** — delete the current block - **Tab / Shift + Tab** — indent or outdent a list item - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + T** — quick-add a task - **Cmd/Ctrl + Enter** — toggle the current checkbox between done and not done - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + M** — add a comment to the selection - **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + H** — open version history Remap most shortcuts under **Settings → Preferences → Keyboard**. A handful of system shortcuts (the command palette and search) are fixed and cannot be changed. Reset to defaults at any time from the same screen.

This page collects the hard limits and quotas in Tessera in one place. Where a limit depends on your plan, all four tiers are listed. Storage covers attachments, images, and files. Notes' text content is effectively unlimited and does not count toward storage. - **Free** — 2 GB total - **Plus** — 50 GB total - **Team** — 100 GB pooled per member - **Business** — 1 TB pooled per member Pooled storage means the whole organization shares the sum of every member's allowance. Maximum size for a single uploaded file: - **Free** — 50 MB - **Plus** — 250 MB - **Team** — 250 MB - **Business** — 500 MB Supported image formats are PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP, and SVG. Any file type can be attached, but only images render inline. - **Notes per account:** 1,000 on Free; unlimited on all paid plans. - **Single note size:** up to 5 MB of text content. - **Table size:** up to 50 columns; rows are unlimited. - **Folder nesting:** up to 8 levels deep. - **Pinned items:** up to 15 per person. - **Shared spaces:** 2 on Free; unlimited on paid plans. - **Members per shared space:** 3 on Free; 250 on Team and Business. - **Boards per space:** up to 20. - **Invitation links** expire after 7 days. - **Public links** are read-only and can be given an expiry date and password. - **Export download links** expire after 72 hours. Per token, per minute: 60 (Free), 120 (Plus), 600 (Team), 1,200 (Business). List endpoints return up to 100 items per page. See the REST API reference for details. - **Trash:** 30 days. - **Version history:** 7 days (Free), 90 days (Plus), unlimited (Team and Business). - **Audit log:** 90 days (Team), 1 year (Business). - **Backups:** 35 days. - **Deleted account:** erased after a 30-day grace period. If you bump into a limit, upgrading your plan is the fastest fix. Organizations with unusual needs can discuss custom quotas by contacting **sales@tessera.app**.

Tessera turns a pile of notes into a connected knowledge base through links. Linking is how you build context instead of duplicating it. Type `[[` anywhere in a note to open the link picker, then start typing a note's name. Choose an existing note, or press **Enter** on a new name to create that note on the spot and link to it in one step. You can also select text and press **Cmd/Ctrl + L** to turn the selection into a link. A **backlink** is the reverse of a link: if note A links to note B, then note B shows A in its **Backlinks** section. Backlinks appear in the right-hand context panel and at the bottom of the note. They update instantly and are never edited by hand, so you always see every note that references the one you're reading. Below backlinks, Tessera lists **unlinked mentions** — notes that contain the current note's title as plain text but don't yet link to it. Click **Link** next to any mention to convert it into a real link. Hover over a link to see a **preview card** with the target note's title and first lines. This lets you check a reference without navigating away. Open **Graph** from the command palette to see your notes as a network of dots connected by links. Use it to spot clusters of related work and orphaned notes with no connections. Filter the graph by space or tag, and click any node to jump to that note. The graph view is available on the **Plus**, **Team**, and **Business** plans. Use a **link** (`[[ ]]`) to connect to one specific note. Use a **tag** (`#topic`) to group many notes under a shared label. Open a tag to see every note and task that carries it. A note can have unlimited tags. If a linked note is deleted, the link becomes a **broken link** shown in red. Tessera lists all broken links for a space under **Settings → Spaces → Maintenance** so you can fix or remove them.

Tessera has native apps for iPhone, iPad, and Android that keep your notes and tasks in sync with the web. This page covers installation, offline use, and mobile-only features. The Tessera app requires **iOS 16 or later** or **Android 9 (Pie) or later**. Download it from the **App Store** or **Google Play** and sign in with the same account you use on the web. SSO and passkeys work on mobile. Changes sync automatically over Wi-Fi or cellular, usually within a couple of seconds. A small status dot in the top bar shows sync state: filled when everything is up to date, hollow while syncing. Notes you've opened recently are cached for **offline** reading and editing. You can also mark a space or note as **Available offline** from its menu to keep a full local copy. Edits made offline are queued and merge automatically the next time you're online — Tessera resolves conflicts the same way it does for real-time editing on the web. Tap and hold the app icon, or use the home-screen **Quick capture** shortcut, to jot a note or task in one step. Captures land in your **Inbox** space by default; change the destination in **Settings → Quick capture**. Send content to Tessera from any other app using the system **Share** sheet. Share a link, image, or selected text and choose the space and note to save it into. This is the mobile counterpart to the desktop Web Clipper. Add home-screen **widgets** for **Today's tasks**, a **Quick capture** button, or a pinned note. Widgets come in small, medium, and large sizes on both platforms. Protect the app with **Face ID**, **Touch ID**, or a fingerprint under **Settings → Privacy → App lock**. When enabled, Tessera requires biometric confirmation each time you open the app or return to it after a timeout you choose. Push notifications deliver mentions, assignments, and reminders. Manage them per type under **Settings → Notifications**; mobile respects the same quiet-hours window you set on the web. The graph view and the admin console are not yet available on mobile and open in the mobile web app instead. Board drag-and-drop is supported on tablets but simplified to a status picker on phones.

A note is the basic document in Tessera. The editor is a block-based, Markdown-friendly writing surface designed to stay out of your way. Everything in a note is a **block**: a paragraph, heading, list item, image, table, or embed. Hover the left edge of any block to reveal the **handle** (⋮⋮). Drag the handle to reorder a block, or click it to open the block menu for actions like **Turn into**, **Duplicate**, and **Delete**. Type **/** at the start of an empty line to open the **slash menu** and insert any block type. The editor understands Markdown as you type: - `# `, `## `, `### ` create headings (levels 1–3). - `- ` or `* ` start a bulleted list; `1. ` starts a numbered list. - `[] ` creates a checkbox (a task). - `> ` creates a quote; ``` creates a code block. - `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `~~strikethrough~~`, and `` `code` `` format inline text. Headings build a navigable **outline**. Open it with the outline icon in the top-right of the editor or press **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + O** to jump between sections of a long note. Insert a table from the slash menu. Tables support up to **50 columns**. Drag an image or file directly into a note to attach it. Maximum upload size depends on your plan, from **50 MB** on Free up to **500 MB** on Business. Supported image types are PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP, and SVG. Code blocks offer syntax highlighting for over 100 languages. Set the language from the dropdown in the block's top-right corner. Use **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + C** to copy a block's contents. Open the note's **⋯** menu and choose **Note info** to see word count, character count, and estimated reading time. A single note can hold up to **5 MB** of text content. Press **Cmd/Ctrl + .** to hide the sidebar and context panel for distraction-free writing. Press it again to bring them back. Deleting a note moves it to **Trash**, where it stays for **30 days** before permanent removal. Restore a note from **Trash** in the sidebar; it returns to its original folder.

The **Inbox** is your single feed of everything that needs your attention in Tessera: mentions, replies, task assignments, due-date reminders, and access requests. Click **Inbox** in the sidebar or press **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + I**. A badge shows the number of unread items. Each item links straight to the note, comment, or task that triggered it. - You are **@mentioned** in a note or comment. - Someone **replies** in a thread you're part of. - A task is **assigned** to you or its due date changes. - A **reminder** you set comes due. - Someone **requests access** to a space you administer. - A teammate **shares** a note or space with you. Mark an item as read, or **archive** it to clear it from the unread view while keeping it findable. Use **Mark all as read** to reset the badge. Archived items stay in the **Archived** tab indefinitely. Notifications can reach you through three channels, configured independently under **Settings → Notifications**: - **In-app** — always on; powers the Inbox. - **Email** — batched or immediate, your choice. - **Push** — on the iOS and Android apps. For each event type (mentions, replies, assignments, reminders) you choose which channels deliver it. For example, you might keep mentions on push but route reminders to email only. Instead of one email per event, enable a **daily digest** that summarizes everything from the past day in a single message, delivered at an hour you pick. A **weekly digest** option summarizes activity across all your spaces every Monday. Set **quiet hours** (for example, 7 PM to 8 AM) to pause push and email notifications during that window. In-app notifications still collect in the Inbox so nothing is missed; they simply don't interrupt you. Quiet hours follow your account time zone. Mute an individual space to stop all notifications from it while staying a member. Muting is personal and reversible from the space's **⋯** menu.

The Tessera REST API lets you read and write notes, tasks, and spaces programmatically. This reference covers authentication, conventions, rate limits, and the core endpoints. All endpoints are served under: ``` https://api.tessera.app/v1 ``` The API speaks JSON. Send `Content-Type: application/json` on requests with a body. The API version is part of the path (`/v1`); breaking changes ship under a new version. Authenticate with a **personal access token** sent as a bearer token: ``` Authorization: Bearer tsk_live_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx ``` Create and revoke tokens under **Settings → Developer → API tokens**. Each token is scoped to **read** or **read-write** and to specific spaces. Tokens are shown only once at creation, so store them securely. A request with a missing or invalid token returns **401 Unauthorized**. Rate limits depend on your plan, measured per token per minute: - **Free** — 60 requests/min - **Plus** — 120 requests/min - **Team** — 600 requests/min - **Business** — 1,200 requests/min Every response includes `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` (a Unix timestamp). Exceeding the limit returns **429 Too Many Requests**; respect the `Retry-After` header before retrying. List endpoints are cursor-paginated. Pass `limit` (default **25**, maximum **100**) and `cursor`: ``` GET /v1/notes?limit=50&cursor=eyJpZCI6Im4_8f... ``` The response body includes a `next_cursor` field, which is `null` on the last page. Errors return a JSON body with a stable machine-readable `code`: ```json { "error": { "code": "not_found", "message": "No note exists with that id." } } ``` Common status codes are **400** (bad request), **401** (unauthenticated), **403** (forbidden), **404** (not found), **422** (validation failed), **429** (rate limited), and **500** (server error). - `GET /v1/notes` — list notes. Filter with `space_id`, `tag`, `updated_since`. - `POST /v1/notes` — create a note. Body: `{ "space_id", "title", "content", "folder_id"? }`. `content` is Markdown. - `GET /v1/notes/{id}` — fetch one note, including its Markdown content. - `PATCH /v1/notes/{id}` — update `title`, `content`, or `folder_id`. - `DELETE /v1/notes/{id}` — move a note to Trash. Add `?permanent=true` to delete immediately. - `GET /v1/tasks` — list tasks. Filter with `space_id`, `assignee_id`, `status`, `due_before`. - `POST /v1/tasks` — create a task. Body: `{ "space_id", "title", "status"?, "assignee_id"?, "due_date"?, "priority"? }`. - `PATCH /v1/tasks/{id}` — update any task field, including `status`. - `GET /v1/spaces` — list spaces you can access. - `POST /v1/spaces` — create a space. Body: `{ "name", "icon"? }`. - `GET /v1/search?q=` — full-text search across notes and tasks. Supports the same operators as the in-app search and returns paginated results. - `GET /v1/me` — return the authenticated user and the plan in effect. Create a note with curl: ``` curl https://api.tessera.app/v1/notes \ -H "Authorization: Bearer tsk_live_xxx" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"space_id":"sp_123","title":"Launch checklist","content":"# Launch\n- [ ] Ship"}' ``` A successful create returns **201 Created** with the new note's `id` and `url`.

as the in-app search and returns paginated results. - `GET /v1/me` — return the authenticated user and the plan in effect. Create a note with curl: ``` curl https://api.tessera.app/v1/notes \ -H "Authorization: Bearer tsk_live_xxx" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"space_id":"sp_123","title":"Launch checklist","content":"# Launch\n- [ ] Ship"}' ``` A successful create returns **201 Created** with the new note's `id` and `url`. We announce additions and deprecations on the developer changelog at **tessera.app/developers/changelog**. Additive changes (new fields, new endpoints) can ship without a version bump, so write clients that ignore unknown fields.

Search is the fastest way to find anything in Tessera. It indexes note titles, body text, task titles, comments, and the text inside attached PDFs. Press **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + F** to open full search, or **Cmd/Ctrl + K** for the quick command palette, which searches as you type. Results are ranked by relevance and recency, and update live with each keystroke. The search results page shows up to **100 results** per query. Each result displays the note title, the space it lives in, and a snippet with your search terms highlighted. Refine a search by combining operators: - `in:"Marketing"` — limit to a specific space - `tag:#roadmap` — only notes or tasks with a tag - `from:@dana` — content created by a person - `is:task` or `is:note` — limit to one object type - `created:>2026-01-01` — created after a date - `updated:<2026-06-01` — last edited before a date - `has:attachment` — notes that contain a file - `"exact phrase"` — match an exact phrase Operators can be combined, for example: `in:"Engineering" tag:#bug is:task updated:>2026-06-01`. The left rail of the search page offers click-to-apply filters for **Space**, **Type**, **Tag**, **Assignee**, and **Date range**. Filters and typed operators stack together. Save any query — including its filters — as a **saved search**. Saved searches appear under **Search** in the sidebar and re-run live each time you open them, so a query like "open bugs assigned to me" always reflects the current state. Sort results by **Relevance** (default), **Last edited**, or **Created date** using the control at the top-right of the results page. New and edited content is searchable within a few seconds. Text inside PDFs up to **40 MB** is indexed automatically; scanned image-only PDFs are not searched because Tessera does not perform OCR. Advanced operators such as `from:` and `has:` require the **Plus** plan or higher.

Tessera is built for working together. You can share an entire space, a single note, or a read-only public link, and edit alongside teammates in real time. When two or more people open the same note, you see each other's **cursors** and **selections** with name labels, and edits appear instantly. There is no locking and no merge step — Tessera resolves concurrent edits automatically. Access is controlled by four roles, which apply at the space level and can be overridden on an individual note: - **Viewer** — can read content but not change it. - **Commenter** — can read and add comments, but not edit content. - **Editor** — can read, comment, and edit content. - **Admin** — full Editor rights plus the ability to manage members and space settings. Set a person's role when you invite them, or change it later from **Space settings → Members**. Invite teammates by email from **Space settings → Members → Invite**. Invitation links expire after **7 days**. On the **Free** plan a shared space allows up to **3 members**; the **Team** and **Business** plans allow up to **250 members** per space. A **guest** is someone added to specific notes or spaces but not a full member of your organization. Guests are available on the **Team** and **Business** plans and are useful for clients or contractors. Guests only see what they're explicitly shared and never appear in your member directory. Publish a note to the web with **Share → Create public link**. Anyone with the link can read it without a Tessera account. Public links are **read-only**, can be password-protected, and can be set to expire on a chosen date. Disable a public link at any time to revoke access immediately. Public links never expose other notes, your space, or your member list — only the single published note. Type `@` to mention a teammate in a note or comment. They receive a notification in their Inbox with a direct link to the spot you mentioned them, which makes handing off work quick and traceable. To share one note from an otherwise private space, open **Share** on the note and add people directly. Note-level sharing grants access only to that note, not the surrounding space.

Spaces are the top-level containers in Tessera. Each space holds its own notes, tasks, members, and permissions, which keeps personal work and team work cleanly separated. - **Personal space** — created automatically with your account. Only you can see it unless you share an individual note. You always have exactly one personal space and it cannot be deleted. - **Shared spaces** — created for a team, project, or topic. Members you invite can see everything in the space according to their role. Create a space from the **+** button next to **Spaces** in the sidebar. A space has a name, an icon, and an optional one-line description shown on its home page. Names must be unique within your account and can be up to 60 characters. On the **Free** plan you can have up to **2 shared spaces**. The **Plus**, **Team**, and **Business** plans allow unlimited shared spaces. Inside a space, organize notes with **folders**. Folders can be nested up to **8 levels** deep. Drag a note onto a folder to move it, or use **Move to…** from the note's **⋯** menu. A note lives in exactly one folder at a time. To make a note reachable from several places, use links (`[[Note name]]`) rather than copies — this keeps a single source of truth. Pin up to **15 notes or folders** to the top of the sidebar for quick access. Pins are personal: pinning a note only affects your own sidebar, not your teammates'. Each folder can be sorted by **Title**, **Last edited**, **Created date**, or **Manual** order. Manual order lets you drag notes into a custom sequence. The sort choice is saved per folder and shared with everyone in the space. When a project ends, **archive** the space instead of deleting it. Archived spaces are hidden from the sidebar but remain fully searchable and can be restored at any time from **Settings → Spaces → Archived**. Archiving does not count against your active-space limit on the Free plan. Use **Move to…** and pick a destination space. Moving a note also moves its comments and version history. Links pointing to the moved note are updated automatically so they never break.

Tessera tracks to-dos as **tasks**. A task can live inline inside a note or be managed in a dedicated **board**, and the two stay in sync — checking it off in one place updates it everywhere. - **Inline:** type `[] ` at the start of a line, or use the slash menu and choose **Task**. - **Quick add:** press **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + T** anywhere to capture a task without leaving your current note. - **From a board:** click **+ Add task** in any board column. Every task has these fields: - **Title** (required) - **Status** — `To do`, `In progress`, `Blocked`, or `Done` - **Assignee** — any member of the space - **Due date** — with an optional time and reminder - **Priority** — `Low`, `Medium`, `High`, or `Urgent` - **Tags** — one or more `#tags` A **board** is a Kanban view of the tasks in a space. Columns map to **Status** by default, but you can group by **Assignee**, **Priority**, or **Due date** instead. Drag a card between columns to change its value. Create a board from **+ New → Board**. Each space can hold up to **20 boards**. Filter a board by assignee, tag, priority, or due date. Combine filters with **AND** logic. Save a filtered board as a **view** (for example, "My urgent tasks this week") and switch between views from the tab bar at the top of the board. The **Tasks** entry in the sidebar aggregates every task assigned to you across all spaces, grouped into **Today**, **Upcoming**, **No date**, and **Done**. This is your personal to-do list spanning the whole workspace. Set a task to repeat daily, weekly, monthly, or on a custom schedule. When you complete a recurring task, Tessera automatically creates the next occurrence with the same properties and a new due date. Add a reminder to a due date and Tessera notifies you in the **Inbox**, by email, and via push notification on mobile. Reminders respect your account time zone, set under **Settings → Preferences**.

Templates let you start a note or board from a predefined structure instead of a blank page. They keep recurring documents — meeting notes, project briefs, weekly reviews — consistent across a team. 1. Create a new note and click **Use template** in the empty editor, or open the slash menu and choose **Template**. 2. Pick a template from the gallery or from your space's templates. 3. The note fills with the template's content, which you can then edit freely. Tessera ships with a gallery of built-in templates, including **Meeting notes**, **Project brief**, **Weekly review**, **Product spec**, **Retrospective**, **Daily journal**, and **Reading list**. Built-in templates are available on every plan. Turn any note into a reusable template: 1. Open the note's **⋯** menu and choose **Save as template**. 2. Give the template a name and an optional description. 3. Choose whether it belongs to **you** or to the **space** (shared with all members). Custom templates are available on the **Plus**, **Team**, and **Business** plans. The Free plan can use built-in templates but cannot create custom ones. Templates can include variables that fill in automatically when the template is used: - `{{date}}` — today's date - `{{time}}` — the current time - `{{user}}` — the name of the person creating the note - `{{space}}` — the current space name For example, a meeting-notes template titled `{{date}} — Team sync` produces a note named "2026-06-30 — Team sync" when used. Insert a **template button** inside a note with the `/button` command. Clicking the button appends a block of predefined content — handy for adding a repeating checklist item or a standard section to a long-running document. Manage every template for a space under **Settings → Templates**. From there you can rename, duplicate, reorder, or delete templates, and set one as the **default** that new notes in the space start from.

Common problems and how to fix them. If none of these help, email **support@tessera.app** with your account email and a description of what you saw. 1. Check the sync dot in the top bar — hollow means a sync is in progress. 2. Confirm you're online and not behind a firewall blocking **api.tessera.app**. 3. Reload the app (**Cmd/Ctrl + R** on the web) to force a fresh sync. 4. On mobile, make sure background app refresh is allowed for Tessera. Edits are never lost while offline; they're queued locally and merge when the connection returns. - Double-check your email and that **Caps Lock** is off. - If you use 2FA, make sure your authenticator app's clock is accurate — TOTP codes fail if the device clock drifts more than about 30 seconds. - Use a **recovery code** if you've lost your authenticator. - Reset your password from the **Forgot password?** link on the sign-in page. Reset links expire after **60 minutes**. - Look in **Trash** — it may have been deleted. Notes are recoverable for 30 days. - Use search; the note may have been moved to another folder or space. - Check the space's **Archived** list under **Settings → Spaces**. - Very large notes (near the 5 MB limit) can slow down; split long notes into linked pages. - Disable browser extensions that inject content into pages, which can conflict with the editor. - Clear the app cache from **Settings → Advanced → Clear cache**; this never deletes your content. - Confirm the file is under your plan's upload limit (50 MB on Free, up to 500 MB on Business). - Check that the format is supported (PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP, SVG). - If you're over your storage quota, free space or upgrade — uploads are blocked when storage is full. - Verify their **role** in the space (a Viewer can read but not edit). - For a note shared individually, confirm they were added to that specific note. - Pending invitations expire after 7 days; re-invite if needed. If something looks broken across the whole app, check **status.tessera.app** for current incidents and maintenance windows before filing a report.

Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds a second step to sign-in so that a stolen password alone can't unlock your account. Tessera uses time-based one-time codes from an authenticator app. Tessera supports **TOTP authenticator apps** such as Google Authenticator, 1Password, Authy, and Microsoft Authenticator. Any standard TOTP app works. > **Note:** Tessera does **not** offer SMS text-message codes for 2FA. App-based codes are more secure because they can't be intercepted by SIM-swapping. For a passwordless option, see passkeys in the Account and Security guide. 1. Go to **Settings → Security → Two-factor authentication**. 2. Click **Enable** and scan the QR code with your authenticator app, or enter the setup key manually. 3. Type the 6-digit code from your app to confirm. 4. Save your recovery codes (see below). Once enabled, you'll enter a 6-digit code from your app each time you sign in on a new device. When you enable 2FA, Tessera generates **10 single-use recovery codes**. Store them somewhere safe — a password manager is ideal. Each code works once and lets you sign in if you lose access to your authenticator app. You can regenerate a fresh set at any time under **Settings → Security → Two-factor authentication → Recovery codes**. Regenerating invalidates the previous set immediately. After signing in with 2FA, you can mark a device as **trusted** to skip the code on that device for **30 days**. Trusted-device status is cleared whenever you change your password or sign out everywhere. Use one of your **recovery codes** to sign in, then disable and re-enable 2FA with your new device. If you've also lost your recovery codes, contact **support@tessera.app** from your account's verified email address; identity verification can take up to **3 business days**. On **Team** and **Business** plans, an admin can require 2FA for all members under **Admin console → Security**. Members without 2FA are prompted to set it up the next time they sign in and cannot access spaces until they do.

Tessera automatically saves snapshots of every note so you can review how it changed and roll back if needed. You never lose work to an accidental edit. A new **version** is captured automatically when a note has been idle for a few minutes after edits, and at least every hour during active editing. Each version records the author, a timestamp, and the full content at that moment. You can also create a manual snapshot at any time with **Save version** from the note's **⋯** menu. Retention depends on your plan: - **Free** — 7 days of version history - **Plus** — 90 days of version history - **Team** — unlimited version history - **Business** — unlimited version history Versions older than your plan's window are permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. Open the **Version history** tab in the right context panel, or press **Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + H**. The timeline lists every snapshot newest-first. Select a version to preview it; additions are highlighted in green and deletions in red so changes are easy to scan. With a version selected, click **Restore**. The note's current content is replaced by that snapshot — but the act of restoring itself creates a new version, so nothing is ever lost and you can always undo a restore. Click the label on any snapshot to give it a name such as "Approved draft" or "Before redesign." Named versions are pinned to the top of the timeline and are exempt from automatic cleanup, even on the Free plan. Hover any block and choose **Block history** to see who last edited just that block and when. This is useful in shared spaces for understanding who changed a specific paragraph without diffing the whole note. Version history applies to notes. Tasks keep a separate **activity log** showing status changes, reassignments, and due-date edits, visible in the task's detail panel.
